skill.name: business-contact-social-links-skill
skill.description: Finds a company's official website and social media profile links by using BrowserAct workflow templates.
skill.summary: Extract business websites and social profile links from a company name or website URL.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static findings for network access, environment access, and command invocation are mostly true positives for a BrowserAct integration. I found no evidence of prompt injection, hidden exfiltration, or malicious code, but the skill sends user targets and an API key to a third-party service and should carry a warning.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Receives User Targets and Authorization Header
medium_findings.description: The script sends the user-provided company name or website URL to the BrowserAct API and includes the BrowserAct API key in an Authorization header. This appears necessary for the advertised workflow, but it exposes user search targets to a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct requests calls and the Authorization header are visible in the script. The destination is the declared BrowserAct API, so the risk is data exposure to a third party rather than confirmed malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Encourages Direct API Key Disclosure
medium_findings.description: The skill instructions say to ask and wait for the user to provide the BrowserAct API key if the environment variable is missing. The script also tells users to provide the key to the agent, which can expose secrets in chat history or logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant text explicitly asks for the API key when it is not configured. This is a clear secret-handling concern, although it does not prove malicious intent.

low_findings.title: External Command Use Is Documented but Not Internally Dynamic
low_findings.description: The static command-execution findings come from Markdown examples instructing the agent to run a local Python script. The Python script itself does not execute shell commands or construct commands from user input, so command injection evidence was not found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are present in the documentation, but the script receives the target as an argv value and does not invoke a shell. This supports dismissing the static command-execution alerts as a low-risk operational pattern.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Alerts Appear to Be False Positives
low_findings.description: The reviewed files do not contain cryptographic operations or system reconnaissance logic. The static alerts appear to match ordinary Markdown text, output labels, or response-string checks rather than dangerous functionality.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, host inspection, process listing, or filesystem probing. The cited areas contain documentation and API response handling.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Plus Credential Access Combination
dangerous_patterns.description: The static critical heuristic is confirmed as a code pattern because the script reads an API key from the environment and performs outbound requests. The behavior matches the declared BrowserAct integration, so it is not assessed as confirmed malicious.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network and credential-access combination is directly present. Intent appears consistent with the skill purpose, so confidence is lower for maliciousness than for pattern presence.

content.user_title: Find Business Websites and Social Links
content.value_statement: Business researchers often need verified website and social profile links before outreach. This skill uses BrowserAct workflows to find official sites and social channels from a company name or URL.
actual_capabilities: Detects whether the target is a company name or website URL.
Runs a BrowserAct company contact workflow for company names.
Runs a BrowserAct social link scraper workflow for website URLs.
Polls task status until completion, failure, or timeout.
Returns discovered official websites and social media profile links.
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key in the environment.
Sends target company names or URLs to the BrowserAct API.
Accuracy depends on BrowserAct workflow results and public website data.
Does not verify that every returned social profile is currently active.

use_cases.title: Enrich Sales Leads
use_cases.description: Find official websites and social channels for company names before outreach.

use_cases.title: Audit Website Profiles
use_cases.description: Extract social media links from a target website during brand or competitor research.

use_cases.title: Clean CRM Records
use_cases.description: Fill missing website and social profile fields for existing business contacts.

prompt_templates.title: Find One Company
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the official website and social media profiles for OpenAI.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner lookup for one company name

prompt_templates.title: Scrape One Website
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract LinkedIn, Facebook, X, Instagram, YouTube, and TikTok links from https://www.example.com/.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a direct website URL as the target

prompt_templates.title: Compare Several Leads
prompt_templates.prompt: Find official websites and social profile links for these companies, then summarize which profiles were found for each: Acme Corp, Contoso, Globex.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate lead enrichment across multiple companies

prompt_templates.title: Prepare CRM Updates
prompt_templates.prompt: For each target I provide, find canonical website and social profile URLs. Separate high-confidence official links from links that need human review.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for structured CRM cleanup

output: Social profiles found: LinkedIn, X, YouTube
LinkedIn profile URL found on the website.
YouTube channel URL found on the website.

output_examples.input: A short list of company names for CRM enrichment
output: Results group official websites and social profile links by company.
Missing platforms are called out for review.

best_practices: Set BROWSERACT_API_KEY as an environment variable before running the skill.
Review returned links before adding them to outreach or CRM systems.
Use precise company names or canonical website URLs for better matching.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API keys into shared chat logs or documents.
Do not treat every returned profile as verified without human review.
Do not use the skill for private or restricted data collection.

faq.question: What input does this skill accept?
faq.answer: It accepts either a company name or a website URL.

faq.question: Which social platforms can it find?
faq.answer: It looks for LinkedIn, Facebook, X, Instagram, YouTube, and TikTok links.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY to be configured in the environment.

faq.question: Does it send data to an external service?
faq.answer: Yes. It sends the target company name or URL to BrowserAct to run the workflow.

faq.question: Can it process many companies?
faq.answer: The script processes one target at a time. An agent can run it repeatedly for a list.

faq.question: Are the results guaranteed to be official?
faq.answer: No. Results should be reviewed before use in campaigns, reports, or CRM updates.
